qa_pairs.question: What is the deadline for submitting proposals to the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: Proposals must be submitted no later than November 4, 2025, at 11:59 p.m. Eastern Standard Time.

qa_pairs.question: Where should applicants submit their RAC proposals?
qa_pairs.answer: Proposals must be submitted electronically through the CCDB portal, specifically at https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/resource_allocation_competitions/applications.

qa_pairs.question: What is the Principal Investigator's (PI) responsibility regarding the application submission?
qa_pairs.answer: The PI is responsible for ensuring that the application is complete, with all additional documentation uploaded, and that there is no discrepancy between the application document and the online form.

qa_pairs.question: When will applicants be notified about the status of their RAC applications?
qa_pairs.answer: Applicants will be informed regarding the status of their applications via email in spring 2026.

qa_pairs.question: Are pre-submission consultations recommended for RAC applicants?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, if you are applying for the first time to this competition, pre-submission consultations are strongly encouraged.

qa_pairs.question: By when should pre-submission consultations take place?
qa_pairs.answer: Consultations should happen prior to October 31, 2025, to allow adequate time for support by technical staff.

qa_pairs.question: What are the goals of a pre-submission consultation?
qa_pairs.answer: The goals are to determine whether the resources needed for your project justify submitting a RAC application, verify the project's eligibility to the right application process (RPP vs RRG), and provide technical assistance with the calculation of the resources needed.

qa_pairs.question: How can I schedule a pre-submission consultation?
qa_pairs.answer: To schedule a consultation, you can send an email to allocations@tech.alliancecan.ca or contact your regional support team.

qa_pairs.question: What is required to submit RAC applications online?
qa_pairs.answer: Users must log in using an existing Alliance account or register for a new one on the CCDB portal.

qa_pairs.question: What is the CCV requirement for RAC applications?
qa_pairs.answer: An up-to-date Canadian Common CV (CCV) is required for the peer review process for both the PI and all Co-PIs.

qa_pairs.question: What is the consequence of not providing an updated CCV?
qa_pairs.answer: Failing to provide an updated CCV will negatively impact the overall score of the submitted application.

qa_pairs.question: Can I get an extension for the submission deadline if my CCV is missing?
qa_pairs.answer: No, deadline extensions due to missing CCVs are not possible.

qa_pairs.question: What are the guiding principles of the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: The RAC is guided by principles ensuring fair consideration through peer and technical review, awarding resources based on appropriateness and efficient use, and sharing challenges from resource shortages among all applicants.

qa_pairs.question: Who conducts the technical review for RAC applications?
qa_pairs.answer: The technical review is conducted by technical experts of the Federation.

qa_pairs.question: What tasks do technical reviewers perform?
qa_pairs.answer: Technical reviewers ensure the appropriate system is requested and software is available, evaluate application efficiency and scalability, identify groups needing optimization help, identify discrepancies, identify special software requirements, and provide a technical opinion on the reasonability of the request.

qa_pairs.question: What is required of applicants if technical staff request additional information during the technical review?
qa_pairs.answer: Applicants are expected to respond to requests from the technical team within 48 hours to ensure their application can progress beyond the technical review.

qa_pairs.question: Who conducts the peer review for RAC applications?
qa_pairs.answer: All applications submitted to the RAC are peer reviewed and scored by peer reviewers.

qa_pairs.question: What must peer reviewers agree to before accessing applications?
qa_pairs.answer: Peer reviewers are required to accept the Alliance's Non-Disclosure Agreement and Conflict of Interest Policy prior to accessing any application.

qa_pairs.question: On what basis is the peer review score determined?
qa_pairs.answer: The resulting score is based on the feasibility of the computational research project (what research will be done and outcomes delivered), the appropriateness of the resources requested (technical justification), and the likelihood that the resources requested will be efficiently used.

qa_pairs.question: Can Principal Investigators (PIs) choose their peer review committee?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, PIs can select a peer review committee of their choosing; however, applications can be moved to a different committee following consultation with the committee Chairs.

qa_pairs.question: Name some of the peer review committees available.
qa_pairs.answer: Some peer review committees include Astronomy, Astrophysics and Cosmology; Bioinformatics; Chemistry, Biochemistry and Biophysics; Computer Sciences and Mathematics; Engineering; Environmental and Earth Sciences; Humanities and Social Sciences; Nano, Materials and Condensed Matter; Neurosciences, Medical Imaging and Medical Physics; and Subatomic Physics, Nuclear Physics and Space Physics.
